mission.title: Install and Configure Kubeedge on Kubernetes
mission.description: KubeEdge is a Kubernetes Native Edge Computing Framework that extends container orchestration and device management capabilities to edge hosts. By installing KubeEdge, you can manage edge applications and devices seamlessly using Kubernetes APIs, enhancing data processing and privacy at the edge.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the KubeEdge Helm repository to your local Helm configuration to access the KubeEdge charts.
```bash
helm repo add kubeedge https://web-seven.github.io/kubeedge
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install KubeEdge CloudCore
steps.description:
Install the KubeEdge CloudCore component using Helm. This will deploy the necessary resources in the kubeedge namespace.
```bash
helm install cloudcore kubeedge/cloudcore --version 0.1.1 --namespace kubeedge --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the CloudCore pod is running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeedge
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the CloudCore deployment to ensure optimal performance.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment cloudcore --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi -n kubeedge
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
Forward the port to access the CloudCore service locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/cloudcore -n kubeedge 8080:80
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the CloudCore release from the kubeedge namespace.
```bash
helm uninstall cloudcore --namespace kubeedge
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) related to KubeEdge to clean up the cluster.
```bash
kubectl delete crd devices.kubeedge.io
kubectl delete crd nodecontrollers.kubeedge.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the kubeedge namespace to remove all associated resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kubeedge
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration and state of the CloudCore deployment for backup.
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubeedge -o yaml > kubeedge-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the CloudCore installation
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the CloudCore release to the latest version available in the Helm repository.
```bash
helm upgrade cloudcore kubeedge/cloudcore --version 0.1.1 --namespace kubeedge
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the CloudCore pods to ensure they are running the new version.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeedge
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If the CloudCore pods are stuck in Pending state, check for insufficient resources in the cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n kubeedge
```

troubleshooting.title: CloudCore pod crashes frequently
troubleshooting.description:
If the CloudCore pod is crashing, review the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kubeedge
```

troubleshooting.title: Unable to connect to CloudCore service
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot connect to the CloudCore service, ensure that the port forwarding is set up correctly and the service is running.
```bash
kubectl get svc -n kubeedge
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of KubeEdge will have the CloudCore pod running in the kubeedge namespace, and you will be able to access the service through the forwarded port. Resource limits will be applied to ensure optimal performance.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kubeedge https://web-seven.github.io/kubeedge
helm repo update
kubectl set resources deployment cloudcore --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi -n kubeedge

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
